description: A designed official portrait announcement graphic introducing a new department head. The image is a polished studio photograph of a smiling middle-aged man (Markwayne Mullin) with short brown hair and a trimmed reddish-brown beard, wearing a navy suit, white shirt, patterned red/blue tie, and a U.S. flag lapel pin. Behind him are the standard official backdrops: a softly-lit American flag on the left and the blue-and-white Department of Homeland Security seal on the right. Across the lower third, in large bold white sans-serif all-caps, is the name 'MARKWAYNE MULLIN', and beneath it in smaller spaced white capitals 'SECRETARY of HOMELAND SECURITY' (with 'of' in italics). The clean, formal corporate/government layout is a welcome/appointment card. Messaging technique: standard institutional branding to announce and legitimize a new DHS Secretary; flag-and-seal staging conveys authority and continuity. Posted by ICE welcoming the new secretary. Target audience: general public, agency personnel, press.
notable_text: MARKWAYNE MULLIN; SECRETARY of HOMELAND SECURITY
